item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When life give you lemon, stuff your brother in it.

item.title: Re: I added a clicker for navigation
item.content:encoded: You should probably just let Wiki-Walk do it. You left off the footnoteblock.

item.title: I added a clicker for navigation
item.content:encoded: For navigation purposes I've added a clicker to SCP-3423's page and SCP-3425's page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: id like to know, is SCP-3424-1 himself anomalous in nature, or is he being affected by SCP- 3424?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You would be correct indeed. Nice catch!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Would I be correct in thinking that strange things are afoot at the ██████ █?

(Not sure how obscure that reference is, what with the redaction, but it fits and is a place one could conceivably obtain a lemon.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first I was sad that this only had 32 (now 33) upvotes.

Then I saw the date - Sept. 10. Gives me real hope, this should definitely have hundreds of upvotes, not dozens. Really, really nicely done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow. This is a good one. It's scary and sad and funny and whafuck? all at the same time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really well done characters and no grammar or spelling errors that I saw.
It reminds me of lemon-head from Pink Guy.
(faint "im a lemon" in the distance)
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would recommend putting the discovery of the skip after the actual description or in an addendum

Also is the lemon still in a containment locker in the procedures? Wording gets a little confusing :<

overall nice article

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't agree with your assertion, but I think it's a fair point. I like the blinking and snapping combo because I like the idea of this SCP being centered around a teenager trying to help his brother who had no idea what he was getting into. It does make the logs more complex, maybe a tad more arduous - but I was trying to hammer home the idea that by having a complex combo and literally blinking in and out of existence at the whims of the people interviewing him, this kid was going to have a tougher road to hoe, and all for art that needed to be explained way too much. My fear was that if I made it simpler, or made it so he could conceivably stay there until you snapped again, it would undermine that idea. You don't have to snap constantly, but everyone has to blink. It's a situation that sucks, especially for a kid who's still afraid of the dark.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Despite what others are claiming, I'm simply not seeing anything outstanding regarding characterization.

They come across as rather basic and almost aloof - and far too much of the interactions are weighed down with the complexity of forcing the manifestation. A different approach to streamline this (perhaps, ignoring the blinking aspect, and rely solely on the snapping), may go a ways to make the conversations flow easier.

While I do like the 'wham line' - it comes across a a silly wink and a nudge - this lacks the weight or poignancy I think it could due to the buildup towards that point being a series of goofy toying with the language barrier/his situation during his inexistence…and everything regarding him as a person and the overall point is just lumped in at the end when it could have been woven throughout.

This work has potential to make a statement, but it's not effective at it in its current state. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Poor guy. This is anart, political agitprop, and existential horror in one small yellow package.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this a lot more than I would have expected to - the interviews were what got me to +1. All the characterization was great, especially that poor kid in the lemon. "How else do you get a servant's attention?"

The pretext for turning him into a fruit in the first place is kind of thin, but that fits perfectly with my headcanon for AWCY and what they would do to a political artist who got caught up with them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this. Excellent character development and much more poignant use of AWCY than is generally done. A hearty +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Esto no es un limón.
OR
Don't turn a teenager into a lemon and expect everyone to get it.
